large_multi_agent_system.coordination_protocol: attempt routing + trace capture + skill distillation + proof-gated selection + network propagation + governance twin release

proof_gates.name: Holdout success uplift over static coordination
Value capture uplift over static coordination
Skill compounding release curve
proof_gates.required: final value capture > release 0 value capture

public_claim_boundary: This proof is a deterministic benchmark and public mechanism demonstration. It does not claim achieved superintelligence, audited customer ROI, investment returns, financial advice, legal advice, medical advice, employment advice, credit advice, token advice, or Kardashev Type II civilization. It shows a testable mechanism: proof-gated skill propagation can outperform static or unverified coordination under the stated benchmark assumptions.

skills_used.input: task, agent, role quorum, outcome, risk event, value outcome
skills_used.name: Attempt Trace Capture
skills_used.output: structured trace with provenance and measurable result
skills_used.purpose: Capture every agent attempt as reusable evidence instead of losing the operational lesson after completion.
skills_used.verifier: trace schema, deterministic seed, holdout isolation check

skills_used.input: high-quality traces and task requirement vectors
skills_used.name: Skill Distillation
skills_used.output: candidate skill with domain, vector, cost, and risk profile
skills_used.purpose: Compress successful repeated work patterns into candidate reusable skills.
skills_used.verifier: candidate must improve validation tasks before propagation

skills_used.input: candidate skill and isolated validation cases
skills_used.name: Proof-Gated Selection
skills_used.output: accepted, rejected, revised, or retired skill decision
skills_used.purpose: Accept only skills that survive validation, holdout, transfer, and risk gates.
skills_used.verifier: uplift threshold, transfer threshold, risk breach ceiling

skills_used.input: uplift, confidence, transfer score, risk penalty, reuse potential
skills_used.name: Gradient Scoring
skills_used.output: signed evolution gradient for each candidate skill
skills_used.purpose: Convert proof outcomes into a gradient that decides what the network should learn next.
skills_used.verifier: positive gradients must outperform static coordination on holdout

skills_used.input: accepted skill, role map, skill compatibility, release gate
skills_used.name: Network Propagation
skills_used.purpose: Share verified skills across the agent network while preventing unverified capability drift.
skills_used.verifier: post-propagation holdout score and negative-control rejection

skills_used.input: skill candidate, release route, policy boundary, rollback path
skills_used.name: Capability Governance Twin
skills_used.output: governed release decision with public receipt
skills_used.purpose: Simulate the release of a new capability before allowing it to influence network routing.
skills_used.verifier: policy gate, rollback gate, verifier coverage gate

skills_used.input: poisoned skills, overfit skills, high-risk shortcuts
skills_used.name: Adversarial Negative Control
skills_used.purpose: Inject plausible but harmful skills to verify that proof, not popularity, decides propagation.
skills_used.verifier: bad skills must receive negative or sub-threshold gradients

skills_used.input: metrics, gates, baselines, skills, evidence hashes
skills_used.name: Executive Receipt Rendering
skills_used.output: JSON receipt, Markdown report, badge, webpage
skills_used.purpose: Turn the proof into a public, non-technical, inspectable artifact.
skills_used.verifier: artifact existence, public boundary, link integrity

tagline: One agent tries. Proof decides. The network evolves.
thesis: Proof Gradient is the selection layer for agent intelligence: agents attempt work, SkillOS extracts reusable skills, proof decides what propagates, and the network evolves through validation-gated capability gradients.
title: Proof Gradient · The Agent Evolution Protocol
